codex_response:
Implemented the new ARC2 package for `642248e4`. The rule is: find the two opposite non-background border lines, then for each `1` add exactly one border-colored cell in the direction of the nearer border, with ties going to the top or left side. The summary hint was consistent with the official examples, so I used it as the starting hypothesis; no new primitive was needed.

Validation passed end to end: official verifier `4/4`, generated verifier `1000/1000`, and the built preview sheet looked structurally correct on spot-check. The generator matches the family by explicitly creating one horizontal or vertical border pair, sparse isolated singleton `1`s kept one insertion-cell away from those borders, and placements on both sides so both output colors appear.
